prompt:
Close-up portrait, 85mm f/1.4, a young East Asian woman in her early 20s seated on the floor beside a low sofa, knees drawn up, back against the wall below the window, authentic daily life photography, natural candid moment.

Almond-shaped dark eyes with a slightly downward cast, gaze aimed at her own hands in her lap, not at camera. High, clear forehead. Naturally straight black hair parted loosely in the center, some strands falling across cheek. Skin tone fair, subsurface scattering visible in diffused side light, faint redness at outer corners of eyes as if from tiredness, natural pores visible, no makeup except faint lip color.

She wears an oversized pale grey cotton long-sleeve shirt, collar wide and slipping to one side, revealing collarbone. Both hands loosely clasped in lap, no gesture or tension. Two or three stray hairs across forehead, natural and unplanned.

Overcast late afternoon window light falling at low angle across the room, cool-warm neutral palette, very low saturation, Fuji Classic Chrome muted rendering, olive-grey shadows, soft ivory skin tones faded slightly. Subtle ISO 400 film grain throughout, photographic texture not CG render smoothness.

Aspect ratio 9:16. No watermark, no text overlay, not cartoon, not digitally painted, not illustration, not anime.
